selected_important_turn.text: Oh I get that! I wanted to make a nice meal for my friends so I was hoping to be able to get extra food. 🙂
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn turns the exchange into an explicit contest over scarce items (each side states a competing need for firewood vs. food). It helped set a zero-sum frame early, shaping subsequent offers and narrowing the conversation to tit-for-tat claims.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: It is purely a self-claim with no attempt to explore the other's constraints or open tradeable possibilities; that makes it escalatory and misses an early chance to reframe toward integrative tradeoffs or gather information that could enable a fairer, higher-value split.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Move from a bare claim to a brief, collaborative inquiry or an integrative suggestion: acknowledge the other's need, ask a quick question about how much firewood they need, or propose concrete trade options (e.g., "I need more food to cook — what amount of firewood would make that fair? Maybe we can split water differently or trade some food for part of your firewood"). Aim to combine explanation of your need with an explicit opening to trade and willingness to find a compromise.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (by opening integrative options and reducing zero-sum framing)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (more information and negotiation around needs enables fairer splits)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve (shows empathy and willingness to collaborate rather than immediately compete)

value2reason.Low: I'd like some additional food just because there are a lot of people in our trip.
value2reason.Medium: I would like extra water because it is supposed to be very hot in the area during our trip and hasn't rained in a while.
value2reason.High: I need more firewood due to having several people join on the trip and needing a bigger fire overall.

value2reason.Low: I have plenty of water, but it would be nice to make a hot drink.
value2reason.Medium: I would like to cool a special meal.
value2reason.High: I would like firewood because I would like an extra fire for cooking.

dialogue.text: Hey!🙂 How are you? What were your plans for the weekend?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Hi. I'm doing great. How are you? I'm planning on going camping.🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm great! Me too! did you have any specific plans? I wanted to make an extra fire to cook with and cook a nice meal.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Yes. I'm actually taking a large group of people. Some friends and family are going and I kind of also wanted a bit of extra firewood. 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Oh I get that! I wanted to make a nice meal for my friends so I was hoping to be able to get extra food. 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need more food to cook — what amount of firewood would make that fair? Maybe we can split water differently or trade some food for part of your firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’m willing to trade 2 firewood for 2 food, but I need at least 1 more firewood to keep the fire going. What do you think about splitting the water 2-1 in your favor?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I’d be okay with 2 firewood for 2 food, but I need at least 1 firewood for myself. How about we split the water 2-1, and I take 1 firewood and 1 food? That way we both get what we need.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm okay with that split. Let me confirm: you take 1 firewood and 1 food, I take 2 firewood and 2 food, and we split the water 2-1 in your favor. That covers all 9 packages.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: That sounds fair. Let me confirm: I take 1 firewood and 1 food, you take 2 firewood and 2 food, and we split the water 2-1 in my favor. All packages are accounted for. Deal!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Deal confirmed. Good luck on the trip!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
